verdicts.evidence: tests re-run green now: `pnpm run test` exit 0 — > @eduagent/source@0.0.0 test C:\Dev\Projects\Products\Apps\eduagent-build > node scripts/doppler-run.mjs run -- nx run-many -t test

claim.ref: Root cause: the homework result screen renders final controls inside a ScrollView with fixed 24px content padding while safe-area padding is on the ScrollView style; on Android navigation-bar devices the last row can sit under system nav. Variants: subject-known action row, auto-detected subject action row, manual/ambiguous subject picker bottom controls, small-height viewport with bottom inset. Acceptance: reserve safe-area-aware scroll content/footer space, keep Retake/Let us go/Continue fully visible and tappable, preserve disabled/enabled readability, add regression coverage for bottom-inset navigation-bar viewport. Regression guard (red-green-revert): apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/homework/camera.test.tsx asserts the result-phase action-row bottom padding is derived from the safe-area inset — RED when padding equals the fixed 24px buffer, GREEN when inset-derived (variant 4 uses a taller inset to prove it) — across all four viewport variants.
claim.text: Root cause: the homework result screen renders final controls inside a ScrollView with fixed 24px content padding while safe-area padding is on the ScrollView style; on Android navigation-bar devices the last row can sit under system nav. Variants: subject-known action row, auto-detected subject action row, manual/ambiguous subject picker bottom controls, small-height viewport with bottom inset. Acceptance: reserve safe-area-aware scroll content/footer space, keep Retake/Let us go/Continue fully visible and tappable, preserve disabled/enabled readability, add regression coverage for bottom-inset navigation-bar viewport. Regression guard (red-green-revert): apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/homework/camera.test.tsx asserts the result-phase action-row bottom padding is derived from the safe-area inset — RED when padding equals the fixed 24px buffer, GREEN when inset-derived (variant 4 uses a taller inset to prove it) — across all four viewport variants.
